sourceMovie.title: The Father
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Father (2020) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Father clicked for you, Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Father clicked for you, 10 Things I Hate About You (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Father?
faq.answer: Try Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind, 10 Things I Hate About You, Collateral Beauty—each shares DNA with The Father while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Father?
faq.answer: The Father (2020) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Father worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
